qa_pairs.question: Can I use Access Control Lists (ACLs) to grant permissions for files or directories that I do not own?
qa_pairs.answer: No, ACLs will not allow you to give away permissions to files or directories that do not belong to you.

qa_pairs.question: Is it advisable to give other users write permission to the top level of my home directory?
qa_pairs.answer: No, it is highly recommended that you never give write permission to other users on the top level of your home directory (/home/G/GROUP/[owner]).

qa_pairs.question: What are the potential risks of giving other users write permission to the top level of my home directory?
qa_pairs.answer: Giving write permission to other users on the top level of your home directory would seriously compromise your privacy and could disable SSH key authentication, among other things.

qa_pairs.question: If I need to allow other users to manipulate files, how should I grant them access within my home directory?
qa_pairs.answer: If necessary, you should make specific sub-directories under your home directory to allow other users to manipulate or access files from those, rather than granting access to the top level.
